variants.transponderChip: PCF7930 / ID33 (E36 EWS2) / PCF7935 / ID44 (E46 EWS3.x)

programming: OBD or bench; aftermarket tools (Autel, Xhorse, Smart Pro) can add keys on EWS systems. All-keys-lost may require EWS bench read.
notes: HU92 2-track blade. E36 (1995-2001) uses EWS2 with ID33 (PCF7930/PCF7931); E46 (1999-2006) transitions to EWS3.x/EWS4.x with ID44/PCF7935. CORRECTED: immobilizer field updated to reflect EWS2 for E36 with ID33 chip per transpondery.com and computersolutions.cn BMW EWS guide. HU92 keyway confirmed by UHS Hardware and transpondery.com.

programming: OBD with CAS programmer. CAS3+ requires Xhorse VVDI2, Autel IM608, Smart Pro, or Lonsdor K518.
notes: HU92 blade used throughout E9x generation. CAS2 uses PCF7936AS; CAS3/CAS3+ uses PCF7945. Smart key adds remote function with same HU92 blade insert. KR55WK49127 US-market frequency confirmed 315 MHz by UHS Hardware, locksmithkeyless.com, and abkeys.com; 433/868 variants exist for European market only.

programming: OBD with supported programmer; bench read possible for all-keys-lost. FEM module must be synchronized.
notes: HU100R emergency blade insert inside smart key fob. PCF7953P (Hitag Pro/ID49) chip cannot be cloned without token (requires authorization). CORRECTED: US-market FCC is YGOHUF5662 at 315 MHz (not YGOHUF5767/433 MHz which is the European/international variant). Confirmed by locksmithkeyless.com, keyinnovations.com, americankeysupply.com, and multiple US aftermarket suppliers. YGOHUF5767 covers EU/Canada 433 MHz market.

programming: OBD + possible bench for all-keys-lost. BDC2 all-keys-lost requires advanced bench tools; Xhorse released BDC2 AKL support May 2026. Autel requires per-VIN subscription fee.
notes: BDC2 (Body Domain Controller generation 2) in all G-series from ~2018-2019+. FCC ID corrected to N5F-ID21A (confirmed by UHS Hardware, abkeys.com, maplekeysupply.ca for G20/G30/G05/G01); prior entry NBG1DGNG1 is unconfirmed/inapplicable to G-chassis BDC2 cars. Significantly harder than FEM; bench read typically required for all-keys-lost. Locksmith scope mostly limited to add-key if owner has one working key.

programming: OBD with FEM-compatible programmer.
notes: Same FEM/HU100R/ID49 architecture as F30 3 Series. CORRECTED: US-market FCC is YGOHUF5662 at 315 MHz (YGOHUF5767 is 433 MHz European variant). F82/F83 M4 may vary by build date.

programming: OBD with CAS programmer.
notes: E60 uses same HU92 blade as E90; transitions from CAS2 to CAS3 to CAS3+ across production years. Check build date for exact CAS version. KR55WK49127 US frequency confirmed 315 MHz.

programming: OBD. CAS4/CAS4+ requires token-based authorization for cloning.
notes: F10 5-series transitions to CAS4/CAS4+ with HU100R blade and ID49 chip. CORRECTED: YGOHUF5662 is 315 MHz for US/North America market (not 433 MHz). Confirmed by americankeysupply.com, keyinnovations.com, bestkeysupply.com citing 315 MHz for CAS4 US keys. The 433 MHz variant (YGOHUF5767) is European-market only.

programming: BDC2 — bench read typically required for all-keys-lost. Add-key via OBD if one working key present.
notes: Same BDC2 architecture as G20 3-series. FCC ID corrected to N5F-ID21A (confirmed by UHS Hardware and multiple suppliers for G30/G31 BDC2 keys at 434 MHz).

programming: OBD with CAS3+ compatible programmer.
notes: E84 X1 uses CAS3+ same as E90 late production. HU92 blade. KR55WK49127 US-market frequency 315 MHz confirmed.

programming: OBD with FEM/BDC compatible programmer.
notes: F48 X1 transitions from E84 with new FEM platform and HU100R blade. CORRECTED: US-market FCC is YGOHUF5662 at 315 MHz (not YGOHUF5767/433 MHz which is European). Confirmed per locksmithkeyless.com and americankeysupply.com for US FEM/BDC F-series keys.

programming: OBD or bench for EWS systems.
notes: E83 X3 uses same HU92 / ID44 as E46. Confirmed on UHS Hardware product listing for 2003-2009 X3.

programming: OBD with CAS4-compatible programmer.
notes: F25 X3 uses CAS4 (not FEM). HU100R blade, ID49 chip. Same smart key fob as 5-series F10. CORRECTED: YGOHUF5662 is 315 MHz for US market (not 433 MHz). Confirmed by carandtruckremotes.com (F25 2011-2017 all cite YGOHUF5662), americankeysupply.com, and bestkeysupply.com for CAS4 315 MHz US keys.

programming: BDC2 — bench read for all-keys-lost. Add-key via OBD if working key present.
notes: G01 X3 transitions to BDC2 same as G20 3-series. FCC ID corrected to N5F-ID21A (confirmed by UHS Hardware and abkeys.com for G01/G02 X3/X4 434 MHz BDC2 keys).

programming: OBD or bench for EWS.
notes: E53 X5 confirmed on UHS Hardware product listing (2000-2006 X5 with HU92 / ID44).

programming: OBD with CAS3/CAS3+ programmer.
notes: E70 X5 uses HU92 blade in smart key shell with ID46. KR55WK49127 US-market frequency 315 MHz confirmed.

programming: OBD with FEM/BDC programmer.
notes: F15 X5 uses FEM/BDC with HU100R blade and ID49. CORRECTED: US-market FCC is YGOHUF5662 at 315 MHz (not YGOHUF5767/433 MHz). Confirmed per locksmithkeyless.com and americankeysupply.com for US FEM/BDC X5 keys.

programming: BDC2 bench read for all-keys-lost.
notes: G05 X5 uses same BDC2 as G20/G30. FCC ID corrected to N5F-ID21A (confirmed by UHS Hardware and abkeys.com for G05 BDC2 platform).

programming: OBD with CAS3/CAS3+ programmer.
notes: R56 MINI uses BMW CAS3/CAS3+ architecture with HU92 blade. Same key as BMW E90-era. FCC ID IYZKEYR5602 confirmed 315 MHz by abkeys.com, UHS Hardware, and locksmithkeyless.com for US market.

programming: OBD with FEM/BDC programmer. No onboard self-programming — must use professional equipment.
notes: F56/F60 MINI uses BMW FEM architecture. Emergency blade insert is HU100R (also sold as HU127 — same physical profile). FCC ID NBGIDGNG1 confirmed 434 MHz by locksmithkeyless.com, abkeys.com, and UHS Hardware.

model: C-Class / E-Class / S-Class / GLA / GLC / GLE (FBS3 era)
programming: Requires EIS (Electronic Ignition Switch) read via specialized locksmith tool. FBS3 allows local/offline key programming by qualified locksmith. PIN/SKC often obtainable from EIS with CGDI MB, Autel, or Xhorse VVDI MB.
notes: HU64 blade used across C-Class W202/W203/W204, E-Class W210/W211/W212, S-Class W220/W221, ML-Class W163/W164/W166, GLA X156, GLC X253 (early) — roughly 1998-2015. FBS3 confirmed as dealer-doable by qualified locksmith. IYZ-3312 FCC ID is 315 MHz for US market — confirmed by UHS Hardware OEM listing (2009-2014 FBS3 fobik), bestkeysolution.com, and keylessbest.com. Note: abkeys.com also sells a 433 MHz variant of IYZ-3312 for non-US markets; the US FCC-certified version is 315 MHz only. Smart key variant applies to ~2009-2015 Keyless Go models; transponder-only for 1998-2009.

model: C-Class (W205) / E-Class (W213) / A-Class (W177) / GLA (X247) / GLC (X253) / GLE (W167) / S-Class (W222) (FBS4 era)
programming: DEALER ONLY. FBS4 requires online authentication via encrypted Mercedes servers in Germany. No aftermarket tool can perform FBS4 key addition without factory access. Locksmith scope = lockout only or tow to dealer.
notes: FBS4 introduced ~2015 across W205 C-Class, W213 E-Class, W177 A-Class (2018+), X247 GLA (2020+), X253 GLC (2016+), W167 GLE (2019+), W222 S-Class (2015+). HU64 blade retained but chip/programming is server-encrypted. Some transition-year models (2015-2016) may be FBS3 — verify VIN. FCC ID NBGDM3 confirmed 315 MHz for US market by UHS Hardware and keyinnovations.com.

programming: DEALER ONLY — FBS4 server-based programming.
notes: W176 A-Class US market uses FBS4 from introduction. Smart key FCC ID NBGDM3 confirmed 315 MHz by UHS Hardware and keyinnovations.com (part A-177-905-22-01).

programming: Requires CEM PIN extraction; professional equipment (Autel IM608, VVDI Key Tool, Smart Pro with Volvo module). No DIY self-programming.
notes: HU56 is the standard blade profile designation for P2 platform (S60/S80/V70/XC70/XC90 1999-2014); Silca blank NE66 / Ilco S66NN-P. ID48 (Megamos Crypto) chip confirmed by transpondery.com, UHS Hardware, and locksmithkeyless.com. OEM part 9203132. CORRECTED: LQNP2T-APU remote is 315 MHz for US market (not 433 MHz). Confirmed by UHS Hardware, abkeys.com, keyinnovations.com, and Amazon KEYECU listing all citing 315 MHz for US FCC ID LQNP2T-APU.

programming: Requires CEM data read (Lonsdor K518, Yanhua ACDP with Volvo Module 20). All-keys-lost requires bench access to CEM. OBD add-key possible with working key on some models. No DIY.
notes: SPA-platform XC90 (2016+) uses HU101 blade with DST-AES ID8A chip. Emergency blade HU101 part 31391439. FCC ID YGOHUF8423 confirmed 433 MHz by UHS Hardware OEM listing (PN 32425209) and abkeys.com; locksmithkeyless.com also lists as 433 MHz (YG0HUF8423 variant). Some aftermarket sources cite 434 MHz; UHS Hardware OEM is authoritative.

programming: CEM PIN extraction required; professional equipment.
notes: First-gen XC60 (2009-2017) uses same P3 platform as S80/V70 with HU56 blade (Silca NE66) and ID48. CORRECTED: LQNP2T-APU remote is 315 MHz for US market (not 433 MHz). Confirmed by UHS Hardware OEM listing and abkeys.com.

programming: CEM bench read for all-keys-lost. OBD add-key if working key present.
notes: Second-gen XC60 (2018+) uses SPA platform identical to XC90 2nd gen. HU101 blade, ID8A, FCC YGOHUF8423, 433 MHz confirmed by UHS Hardware OEM listing and abkeys.com.

programming: CEM PIN extraction; professional equipment required.
notes: Second-gen S60 uses P3 platform with HU56 blade (Silca NE66), ID48 chip. Same remote hardware as XC90 first gen. CORRECTED: LQNP2T-APU is 315 MHz for US market (not 433 MHz). Confirmed by UHS Hardware, abkeys.com, locksmithkeyless.com.

programming: CEM bench read for all-keys-lost. Professional equipment required.
notes: Third-gen S60 uses SPA platform. Same key as XC60/XC90 SPA gen. HU101 blade, ID8A chip, 433 MHz confirmed by UHS Hardware OEM listing.

programming: OBD with supported programmer. Some models allow owner self-programming via door lock cycle sequence.
notes: 2007-2018 Outlander uses MIT3/MIT11R keyway with ID46 (Philips Hitag2). Trim split confirmed: ES trim uses RemoteHead key (OUCJ166N / 6370B944, turn-key ignition); SE/LE/GT/SEL trims use Smart proximity key (OUC644M-KEY-N / 8637A817, push-to-start). Smart key is 4-button (Lock/Unlock/Hatch/Panic) at 315 MHz. Transponder-only variant (no remote) also exists for base non-keyless-entry configurations (2007-early). RemoteHead part number corrected to 6370B944 (OEM confirmed by UHS Hardware and northcoastkeyless.com; 6370A159 is an earlier-generation part). Trim split sourced from southsuburbanmitsubishi.com 2018 Outlander ES vs SE comparison and northcoastkeyless.com product listings.

programming: OBD with supported programmer. Some trims allow owner self-programming.
notes: Outlander Sport uses same MIT3 keyway and ID46 chip as Outlander. Trim split confirmed: ES trim uses RemoteHead key (OUCJ166N / 6370B944, turn-key ignition, push-button start NOT included); SE/SE+/GT trims use Smart proximity key (OUC644M-KEY-N / 8637A316, push-to-start standard). Confirmed by 2016 Outlander Sport feature breakdown (SE adds push-button start over ES) and multiple OEM key vendor listings showing both OUCJ166N (2016-2022 Outlander Sport) and OUC644M-KEY-N (2014-2021 Outlander Sport) as simultaneous fitments for different trims. Remote head key part number corrected to 6370B944 (confirmed OEM by UHS Hardware and northcoastkeyless.com). Smart key is 3-button (Lock/Unlock/Panic) at 315 MHz.

immobilizer: Mitsubishi IMMO (ID46 era for DE/base; ID47 for ES+ smart key trims)
programming: OBD with supported programmer. ID47 (Hitag3) smart key requires ID47-capable tool (Lonsdor, OBDSTAR, Autel IM608, Xhorse).
notes: Mirage trim split confirmed: DE (base) trim uses turn-key ignition with RemoteHead key (OUCJ166N / 6370B904, ID46 chip); ES and higher trims use Smart proximity key with push-to-start (OUC003M / 8637B153, ID47/Hitag3 chip). CRITICAL: smart key uses ID47 (Hitag3), NOT ID46 — confirmed by locksmithkeyless.com listing OUC003M with Philips ID47/HITAG3 chip and autorescuetools.com listing OUCG8D-625M-A-HF (earlier 2014-2015 DE remote head key, PCF7961A/ID46). Transponder-only key exists for base non-keyless-entry variant. RemoteHead part corrected to 6370B904 (confirmed OEM by UHS Hardware and locksmithkeyless.com for 2013-2020 Mirage; prior 6370B707 was incorrect). DE vs ES trim push-button start split confirmed by MirageForum.com thread (2015 ES has push-start; DE does not) and search results indicating ES adds keyless push-button ignition over DE.

programming: OBD with ID47-capable programmer. Requires Lonsdor, OBDSTAR, Autel, or Xhorse Mobile-OBD.
notes: Eclipse Cross 2018+ uses ID47 (Hitag3/NCF29A1X) — newer than the ID46 Outlander. Transponder blade: Ilco MIT9PT, Silca 692565 (confirmed by UHS Hardware). Smart key FCC OUCGHR-M013 confirmed 315 MHz for US market by abkeys.com and UHS Hardware; OEM part 8637B639. CORRECTED: Smart key emergency blade keyway is MIT3 (not MIT11R) — confirmed by UHS Hardware OEM listing (test key MIT3), locksmithkeyless.com, and yourcarkeyguys.com emergency blade listing. Note: a separate RemoteHead key variant (OUCJ166N, FCC ID, MIT3 blade, OEM 6370C135) also exists for 2018-2020 non-proximity trims.

immobilizer: Tesla proprietary NFC/BLE (256-bit ECC NIST P-256)
programming: Key cards and phone keys pair via vehicle touchscreen (Settings > Locks > Add). If zero working keys present, vehicle must be transported to Tesla Service Center — no independent locksmith can program keys. Some third parties authorized for limited security Toolbox actions.
programmerHint: Tesla app (owner add-key with working key present)
notes: Tesla Model 3 has NO traditional mechanical keyway, no transponder chip, no blade. Access via: (1) NFC key card (NIST P-256 ECC), (2) smartphone via BLE + NFC, (3) optional keyfob. Locksmith scope = slim jim / lockout assistance only. No aftermarket key cutting or transponder programming applicable. Key card part 113108701J confirmed OEM.

immobilizer: Tesla proprietary NFC/BLE (256-bit ECC NIST P-256)
programming: Same as Model 3 — NFC key card + smartphone BLE. All-keys-lost requires Tesla Service Center. No traditional locksmith key programming possible.
programmerHint: Tesla app (owner add-key with working key present)
notes: Identical key architecture to Model 3. No blade, no chip to clone, no transponder. Locksmith scope = physical lockout assistance only. Key card placed on center console behind cup holder to authenticate. OEM key card part 113108701J.
